body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

The scenario's User-observable signal is `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready` with state NOT sticking in `working` post-interrupt, and the next prompt proving the parser reset via a fresh `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready` arc. Pi binds Escape to "interrupt the in-flight LLM turn" (per its startup banner: `escape interrupt ¬∑ ctrl+c/ctrl+d clear/exit`), so the agent natively supports cancelling a long turn mid-response ‚Äî agent_supports: yes.

HOW irrlicht observes it (and the IMPORTANT structural nuance): pi's transcript parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) emits `turn_done` ONLY when an assistant message carries `stopReason == "stop"`. An ESC-cancelled turn never reaches `stopReason=stop`, so the interrupted turn produces NO `turn_done`. Pi's parser also does NOT set `LastWasUserInterrupt` (it has no `[Request interrupted by user]` marker handling ‚Äî that path is claudecode-specific), so the classifier's rule-3 "user ESC interrupt while working ‚Üí ready" branch does not fire for pi either. The interrupt therefore produces no standalone working‚Üíready transition; the session remains `working` after ESC until the RECOVERY prompt's reply lands a `stopReason=stop` (turn_done), which settles working‚Üíready via classifier rule 2b ("agent finished turn ‚Üí ready"). The user-observable end state is still correct ‚Äî state does not stick in working, it resolves to `ready` once the recovery turn completes ‚Äî and the recovery arc (ready‚Üíworking‚Üíready, all `turn_done`-settled) proves the parser reset cleanly on the next prompt. irrlicht_observes: yes (the spec asserts the observable arc and the no-waiting / no-stick invariant; it does NOT assert a distinct interrupt-triggered transition, which pi does not emit).

Confidence 0.82 (not higher) because the post-interrupt return to `ready` is carried by the recovery turn's `turn_done` rather than by a dedicated interrupt-detection path ‚Äî a future pi version that emits an explicit cancel marker, or a daemon change adding pi interrupt-marker detection, would change the transition shape (an earlier working‚Üíready right after ESC) and warrant a re-record.
caveats: Pi's parser has no `[Request interrupted by user]` marker handling, so classifier rule 3 (user ESC interrupt ‚Üí ready) does not fire for pi. The post-interrupt return to ready is carried by the recovery prompt's `stopReason=stop` (turn_done ‚Üí rule 2b), not by the interrupt itself.

The driver's `interrupt` step sends Escape to the tmux pane and decrements its internal EXPECTED_TURNS (the cancelled turn produces no stop message), so the recipe must NOT follow `interrupt` directly with `wait_turn` ‚Äî it sleeps, then sends the recovery prompt and waits on THAT turn.

Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID; the interactive driver discovers it from the rollout transcript header. A transient `proc-<PID>` pre-session is born ready at launch and bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed transcript appears (same shape as pi/session-start and pi/multi-turn-conversation).

sources.note: stopReason=="stop" ‚Üí turn_done; any other stopReason (incl. a cancelled turn) ‚Üí plain assistant event. No IsUserInterrupt / [Request interrupted by user] handling.

Rule 3 (user ESC interrupt ‚Üí ready) is gated on LastWasUserInterrupt/LastWasToolDenial, which pi never sets. Rule 2b (agent finished turn ‚Üí ready) settles working‚Üíready on the recovery turn's turn_done.
step_interrupt sends Escape and decrements EXPECTED_TURNS; the recovery send + wait_turn pair stays in sync.
